mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and dampening forward S&P 500 returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or high-rate periods show statistically indistinguishable returns from low-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Sahm-style rise events show no significant negative return skew (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint level of FEDFUNDS relative to CPIAUCSL-implied inflation determines the real policy rate; when nominal rates substantially exceed trailing inflation, real tightness restrains equity valuations, producing negative forward returns that a single rate or inflation series alone would not capture.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY change proxy) fails to add incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone, measured by no improvement in out-of-sample Rank IC across 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand that leads broader economic activity by several months, translating into negative S&P 500 forward returns specifically during periods of restrictive monetary policy when housing is most sensitive.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, declining HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.03, or returns following HOUST declines are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect strengthening real economic output and corporate revenue momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week as markets incorporate improving supply-side fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth accelerates above trend, it signals renewed inflationary pressure that raises expectations of continued Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month until the inflation reading normalizes.
failure_condition: Triggered CPI acceleration episodes show 20-day forward SPX returns positive in more than 55% of instances, or no significant difference from non-triggered baseline returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in payroll growth (PAYEMS) alongside a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) constitutes a joint labor-market inflection that independently neither series confirms as strongly, and this combined weakening predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as recession risk repricing occurs.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise events fail to produce statistically significant negative 60-day forward returns relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week, but this effect is only meaningful during late-cycle expansion when consumer spending is the dominant growth driver rather than early recovery phases.
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes, RSAFS growth shows Rank IC below 0.02 against 5-day forward SPX returns over any rolling 2-year window, or positive RSAFS surprises fail to outperform baseline returns (p > 0.05).
